Feature.title: Feature
Feature.description: Represents a key feature of the web hosting service, displayed to users on the AmarShebaHost website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Feature entity.

title.description: The name of the feature (e.g., 'High Speed Servers', '99.9% Uptime Guarantee').

description.description: A brief, explanatory description of the feature's benefit or functionality.

iconIdentifier.description: A string representing the icon to be displayed for the feature (e.g., an icon library class name or a URL to an SVG).

displayOrder.description: The numerical order in which the feature should be displayed on the website.

PricingPlan.title: PricingPlan
PricingPlan.description: Defines a web hosting pricing plan offered by AmarShebaHost, including its name, cost, and included features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PricingPlan entity.

name.description: The marketing name of the pricing plan (e.g., 'Basic', 'Standard', 'Premium').

description.description: A short description or tagline summarizing the plan's purpose or target audience.

price.description: The numerical price of the plan.

currency.description: The currency of the price (e.g., 'BDT' for Bangladeshi Taka, 'USD' for United States Dollar).

features.description: A list of key features or benefits included in this specific pricing plan.

isPopular.description: A flag indicating if this plan should be highlighted on the website as the 'most popular' option.

callToActionUrl.description: The URL for the 'Buy Now' button associated with this plan, directing users to purchase.

displayOrder.description: The numerical order in which the pricing plan should be displayed on the website.

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Stores customer reviews and ratings to build trust and credibility for AmarShebaHost.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

customerName.description: The full name of the customer who provided the testimonial.

customerTitle.description: The customer's title or affiliation (e.g., 'CEO of ABC Corp', 'Small Business Owner').

reviewText.description: The actual text content of the customer's review or feedback.

rating.description: The customer's rating, typically on a scale of 1 to 5 stars.

datePosted.description: The date and time when the testimonial was posted or recorded.

isApproved.description: A flag indicating whether the testimonial has been approved by an administrator for public display.

displayOrder.description: The numerical order in which the testimonial should appear on the website.

NavigationLink.title: NavigationLink
NavigationLink.description: Represents a menu item or link within the website's navigation, allowing for configurable links and hierarchical structures via an admin panel.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NavigationLink entity.

title.description: The display text for the navigation link (e.g., 'Home', 'Pricing', 'Domain Registration').

url.description: The destination URL for the navigation link.

location.description: Specifies where the link is displayed on the website (e.g., 'header_main', 'footer', 'domain_submenu').

displayOrder.description: The numerical order of the link within its specific location or among its sibling menu items.

parentId.description: Reference to another NavigationLink if this is a sub-item in a hierarchical menu. Null for top-level menu items. (Relationship: NavigationLink 1:N NavigationLink)

WebsiteContentBlock.title: WebsiteContentBlock
WebsiteContentBlock.description: A generic entity for storing configurable text content or blocks used throughout the website, such as headlines, descriptions, or footer text, manageable via an admin panel.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WebsiteContentBlock entity.

key.description: A unique programmatic key to identify this content block (e.g., 'hero_headline', 'about_us_description', 'footer_copyright_text').

value.description: The actual content of the block, which can be plain text, HTML, or markdown.

contentType.description: Specifies the type of content stored (e.g., 'plaintext', 'html', 'markdown') to aid in rendering and selecting the appropriate editor in an admin panel.

description.description: A descriptive explanation of the purpose or typical location of this content block on the website.

definition.description: Collection to store administrative roles. The existence of a document here, where the document ID matches a user's UID, grants that user admin privileges across the application. This is a DBAC implementation for global roles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is an administrator.

definition.description: Stores key features of the web hosting service. Documents are publicly readable but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: featureId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific feature.

definition.description: Contains details for various web hosting pricing plans. Documents are publicly readable but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific pricing plan.

definition.description: Stores customer testimonials. Documents are publicly readable only if 'isApproved' is true, and only modifiable by administrators. This supports QAPs for public lists.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific customer testimonial.

definition.description: Manages all navigation menu items and links across the website, including hierarchical structures (via parentId) and dynamic URLs. Documents are publicly readable but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: linkId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific navigation link.

definition.description: A generic collection for storing various configurable text or HTML content blocks used throughout the website (e.g., headlines, descriptions). Documents are publicly readable but only modifiable by administrators.

params.name: blockId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific content block.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles. The primary focus is on managing public-facing website content (Features, Pricing Plans, Testimonials, Navigation Links, and Website Content Blocks) and providing an administrative interface for modifying this content.

**Authorization Independence:** This design explicitly avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies by ensuring that access to any document does not rely on fetching data from a parent document using `get()`. For instance, `NavigationLink` documents can reference a `parentId` for data structuring (e.g., creating sub-menus), but this `parentId` is a data relationship, not an authorization dependency. All documents in the primary content collections (`/features`, `/pricingPlans`, `/testimonials`, `/navigationLinks`, `/websiteContentBlocks`) are either publicly readable or their access is determined solely by the document's own fields (like `isApproved` for `Testimonial`) or the user's global administrative role.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
*   For collections like `/features`, `/pricingPlans`, `/navigationLinks`, and `/websiteContentBlocks`, all documents within these collections are intended for public display. Therefore, a `list` operation is inherently secure as all retrieved documents are meant to be public. Security rules will simply allow public read access.
*   For `/testimonials`, the design includes an `isApproved` field. Security rules will enforce that only documents with `isApproved: true` are publicly readable. While clients will typically query `where('isApproved', '==', true)`, the security rule acts as a final gatekeeper, ensuring that any `list` operation (even a malformed one trying to read unapproved testimonials) will only return approved documents to non-admin users, thus upholding the QAP principle.

**DBAC and Admin Access:** Administrative access for modifying any of these content collections will be managed via a dedicated `roles_admin` collection (`/roles_admin/{uid}`). The mere existence of a document in this collection with a user's `uid` as its ID grants them administrative privileges. This avoids custom claims and simplifies security rules, making admin checks a straightforward `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` operation for write permissions across all content-management collections.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`/features`, `/pricingPlans`, `/testimonials`, `/navigationLinks`, `/websiteContentBlocks`) maintains a homogeneous security posture. For example, all `Feature` documents are public read, admin write. This simplifies rule writing significantly. The `NavigationLink` entity's `location` field allows for conceptual segregation (e.g., 'header_main', 'domain_submenu') without requiring separate physical collections, as the access patterns remain the same.

**User Request Fulfillment:** The user's request to have dynamic, admin-manageable links for "domain" and "hosting reseller" options, including sub-titles and URLs, is directly addressed by the `NavigationLink` entity. The `title` and `url` fields are editable by administrators. The `parentId` field facilitates hierarchical menus (e.g., 'Domain' as a parent, with 'Domain Registration', 'Domain Pricing', 'Domain Transfer' as children). The `WebsiteContentBlock` provides generic, key-value storage for other dynamic text content like headlines or descriptions, also manageable by admins.
